page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.r7.4xlarge consistently in the average performance tier. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1864.49 and a multi-core score of 15504.4, complemented by a PassMark CPU score of 20751.25. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 849.57 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 59.04 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance demonstrates a static web serving throughput of 20.97 GB/sec and processes 15.46 million Redis SET operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU handles small-model LLM text generation at 254.84 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak 30.95 tokens per second for medium-model prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7.4xlarge provides a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable execution times for enterprise applications. This instance is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and moderate web serving operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, it delivers stable, mid-tier performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 20751.25 and a Redis throughput of 15.46 million SET operations per second. Memory bandwidth reaches 849.57 GB/sec for small cached blocks. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is highly efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe disks. It is recommended for in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring predictable compute performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated memory-optimized virtual server featuring high RAM density and predictable compute performance for enterprise caching and database workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Delivers 15.46 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmarks
